TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: tabakiay apalu, tabakiay apalo ś≥ĘÁĺÖŤúú u satabakiay a heci i kanatal. maka 10-20m talakaw. baeket ku heci maka 5-20kg. kagak kakuniza subal : salaedan :ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae panan :ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upiz : mata :ŤĖĒŤĖáÁõģ Rosales sapamat :ś°ĎÁßĎ Moraceae mikitinay :ś°āśú®ŚĪ¨ Artocarpus hicahicaay :ś≥ĘÁĺÖŤúú A. heterophyllus u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika The jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus), also known as jack tree, fenne, jakfruit, or sometimes simply jack or jak, is a species of tree in the fig, mulberry, and breadfruit family native to South India. The jackfruit tree is well suited to tropical lowlands, and its fruit is the largest tree-borne fruit, reaching as much as 35 kg (80 lb) in weight, 90 cm (35 in) in length, and 50 cm (20 in) in diameter.A mature jackfruit tree can produce about 100 to 200 fruits in a year. The jackfruit is a multiple fruit, composed of hundreds to thousands of individual flowers, and it is the fleshy petals that are eaten. The jackfruit tree is a widely cultivated and popular food item throughout the tropical regions of the world. Jackfruit is the national fruit of Bangladesh.
S.PHON: tabakiaj apar~…ĺu, tabakiaj apar~…ĺo *** u satabakiaj a ńß…ô ¶i i kanatar~…ĺ. maka **-**m tar~…ĺakaw. ba…ôk…ôt ku ńß…ô ¶i maka *-**kg. kagak kakuniza subar~…ĺ : sar~…ĺa…ôdan :*** pr~…ĺanta…ô panan :***** magnor~…ĺiopńßjta upiz : mata :*** *osar~…ĺ…ôs sapamat :** mora ¶…ôa…ô mikitinaj :*** arto ¶arpus ńßi ¶ańßi ¶aaj :*** a. ńß…ôt…ôropńßjr~…ĺr~…ĺus u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit (arto ¶arpus ńß…ôt…ôropńßjr~…ĺr~…ĺus), ar~…ĺso known as ja ¶k tr…ô…ô, f…ônn…ô, jakfruit, or som…ôtim…ôs simpr~…ĺj ja ¶k or jak, is a sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tr…ô…ô in tńß…ô fig, mur~…ĺb…ôrrj, and br…ôadfruit famir~…ĺj nati*…ô to soutńß india. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô is w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ suit…ôd to tropi ¶ar~…ĺ r~…ĺowr~…ĺands, and its fruit is tńß…ô r~…ĺarg…ôst tr…ô…ô-born…ô fruit, r…ôa ¶ńßiŇč as mu ¶ńß as ** kg (** r~…ĺb) in w…ôigńßt, ** ¶m (** in) in r~…ĺ…ôŇčtńß, and ** ¶m (** in) in diam…ôt…ôr.a matur…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô ¶an produ ¶…ô about *** to *** fruits in a j…ôar. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit is a mur~…ĺtipr~…ĺ…ô fruit, ¶ompos…ôd of ńßundr…ôds to tńßousands of indi*iduar~…ĺ fr~…ĺow…ôrs, and it is tńß…ô fr~…ĺ…ôsńßj p…ôtar~…ĺs tńßat ar…ô …ôat…ôn. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô is a wid…ôr~…ĺj ¶ur~…ĺti*at…ôd and popur~…ĺar food it…ôm tńßrougńßout tńß…ô tropi ¶ar~…ĺ r…ôgions of tńß…ô worr~…ĺd. *a ¶kfruit is tńß…ô nationar~…ĺ fruit of baŇčr~…ĺad…ôsńß.
S.FORM: tabakiay apalu, tabakiay apalo ś≥ĘÁĺÖŤúú u satabakiay a heci i kanatal. maka 10-20m talakaw. baeket ku heci maka 5-20kg. kagak kakuniza subal : salaedan :ś§ćÁČ©ÁēĆ Plantae panan :ŤĘęŚ≠źś§ćÁČ©ťĖÄ Magnoliophyta upiz : mata :ŤĖĒŤĖáÁõģ Rosales sapamat :ś°ĎÁßĎ Moraceae mikitinay :ś°āśú®ŚĪ¨ Artocarpus hicahicaay :ś≥ĘÁĺÖŤúú A. heterophyllus u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika The jackfruit (Artocarpus heterophyllus), also known as jack tree, fenne, jakfruit, or sometimes simply jack or jak, is a species of tree in the fig, mulberry, and breadfruit family native to South India. The jackfruit tree is well suited to tropical lowlands, and its fruit is the largest tree-borne fruit, reaching as much as 35 kg (80 lb) in weight, 90 cm (35 in) in length, and 50 cm (20 in) in diameter.A mature jackfruit tree can produce about 100 to 200 fruits in a year. The jackfruit is a multiple fruit, composed of hundreds to thousands of individual flowers, and it is the fleshy petals that are eaten. The jackfruit tree is a widely cultivated and popular food item throughout the tropical regions of the world. Jackfruit is the national fruit of Bangladesh.
S.PHON: tabakiaj apar~…ĺu, tabakiaj apar~…ĺo *** u satabakiaj a ńß…ô ¶i i kanatar~…ĺ. maka **-**m tar~…ĺakaw. ba…ôk…ôt ku ńß…ô ¶i maka *-**kg. kagak kakuniza subar~…ĺ : sar~…ĺa…ôdan :*** pr~…ĺanta…ô panan :***** magnor~…ĺiopńßjta upiz : mata :*** *osar~…ĺ…ôs sapamat :** mora ¶…ôa…ô mikitinaj :*** arto ¶arpus ńßi ¶ańßi ¶aaj :*** a. ńß…ôt…ôropńßjr~…ĺr~…ĺus u siwkaj nu nipar~…ĺuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…ĺuma mar~…ĺańß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…ĺaŇč pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…ĺa ¶ur~…ĺ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sauŇčaj misapaju Ē~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…ĺit nu zuma a kamu-ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit (arto ¶arpus ńß…ôt…ôropńßjr~…ĺr~…ĺus), ar~…ĺso known as ja ¶k tr…ô…ô, f…ônn…ô, jakfruit, or som…ôtim…ôs simpr~…ĺj ja ¶k or jak, is a sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tr…ô…ô in tńß…ô fig, mur~…ĺb…ôrrj, and br…ôadfruit famir~…ĺj nati*…ô to soutńß india. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô is w…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ suit…ôd to tropi ¶ar~…ĺ r~…ĺowr~…ĺands, and its fruit is tńß…ô r~…ĺarg…ôst tr…ô…ô-born…ô fruit, r…ôa ¶ńßiŇč as mu ¶ńß as ** kg (** r~…ĺb) in w…ôigńßt, ** ¶m (** in) in r~…ĺ…ôŇčtńß, and ** ¶m (** in) in diam…ôt…ôr.a matur…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô ¶an produ ¶…ô about *** to *** fruits in a j…ôar. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit is a mur~…ĺtipr~…ĺ…ô fruit, ¶ompos…ôd of ńßundr…ôds to tńßousands of indi*iduar~…ĺ fr~…ĺow…ôrs, and it is tńß…ô fr~…ĺ…ôsńßj p…ôtar~…ĺs tńßat ar…ô …ôat…ôn. tńß…ô ja ¶kfruit tr…ô…ô is a wid…ôr~…ĺj ¶ur~…ĺti*at…ôd and popur~…ĺar food it…ôm tńßrougńßout tńß…ô tropi ¶ar~…ĺ r…ôgions of tńß…ô worr~…ĺd. *a ¶kfruit is tńß…ô nationar~…ĺ fruit of baŇčr~…ĺad…ôsńß.
